Scope stays shell-only and limited to the tx interface harness plus directly-related local validation if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentWolf","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:34:03.196295Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:39:56.800841Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:39:56.798216Z","close_reason":"Added remote path-topology preflight to tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh; validated with bash -n, shellcheck, git diff --check, and direct collector/workers.toml checks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","e2e-tests","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:34:03.196295Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.5","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Fail-closed rch guards for replay smoke harnesses","description":"Bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1. Scope: harden the replay-oriented E2E shell harness cluster so heavy cargo tests never run locally by accident. Target files: tests/e2e/test_replay_provenance.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_shadow_rollout.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_post_incident.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_side_effect_barrier.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_interface_parity.sh. Requirements: add rch presence/probe/smoke preflight, route cargo through rch exec with a repo-local target dir, detect fail-open signatures like [RCH] local / running locally / SSH/socket failures, and fail closed with clear logs. Keep scope to these harnesses only; no product-runtime code changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyBridge","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:40:00.256993Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:40:00.256993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ops","rch","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:40:00.256993Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.6","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Add remote-stall fail-closed guards to replay smoke harnesses","description":"Bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1. Scope: harden the replay-oriented E2E shell harness cluster so stalled remote rch executions fail closed instead of hanging indefinitely. Target files: tests/e2e/test_replay_provenance.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_shadow_rollout.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_post_incident.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_side_effect_barrier.sh, tests/e2e/test_replay_interface_parity.sh. Requirements: add portable timeout/gtimeout discovery, enforce per-step remote timeout via RCH_STEP_TIMEOUT_SECS, capture rch queue snapshots on timeout, emit explicit RCH-REMOTE-STALL diagnostics/artifacts, and keep scope to these harnesses only; no product-runtime code changes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:39:21.414271Z","created_by":"JadeJay","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:39:21.414271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:39:21.414271Z","created_by":"JadeJay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3][support] Enforce rch for heavy cargo SendText policy path","description":"Implement a bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3: add reusable heavy-cargo/rch classification helpers in build_coord and enforce require-approval for non-human SendText attempts that invoke heavy cargo commands without an rch wrapper. Keep scope to policy/build_coord/tests only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteFox","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-14T21:18:49.459475Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2036,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","author":"WhiteFox","text":"Implemented the bounded policy/build_coord slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs`: added `command_uses_rch`, `is_heavy_cargo_command`, `requires_rch_offload`, and `recommended_rch_prefix()`; the parser now handles env-prefixed commands and `rch exec -- cargo ...` wrappers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`: added `policy.rch.heavy_compute` so non-human `SendText` requests invoking heavy cargo without `rch` now return `RequireApproval` with an `rch` prefix recommendation.
- Added focused unit tests covering wrapped/unwrapped heavy cargo detection and policy decisions.

Validation:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` -> remote success on `vmi1149989`.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` in 2m07s (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Initial remote compile exposed an implementation bug (`shell_words::split` unresolved in `build_coord.rs`); fixed by switching to existing `shlex::split`, then reran the remote check successfully.

Remaining blocker:
- Targeted remote `cargo test` commands for the new unit tests are currently blocked by worker topology drift: scheduler repeatedly sends them to `vmi1227854`, which exits 101 because `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml` is missing on that worker. Implementation is complete; remote test execution is infra-blocked rather than code-blocked.
","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33Z"},{"id":2318,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","author":"SapphireCreek","text":"Support slice in 3 mission E2E harnesses: tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_5_7_contract_golden.sh, tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_6_8_impact_attribution.sh, and tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_7_4_perf_scalability.sh. Changes: added fail-closed rch preflight (workers probe + cargo check --help smoke), routed heavy cargo through rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=..., added local-fallback detection for [RCH] local / running locally / SSH socket-path failures, and removed local rm -rf target cleanup from these harnesses. Verification: bash -n PASS; git diff --check PASS; TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help => [RCH] remote vmi1149989; live run of test_ft_1i2ge_5_7_contract_golden.sh cleared rch probe + smoke and entered remote cargo check on vmi1149989 (see tests/e2e/logs/ft_1i2ge_5_7_20260312_115214.{jsonl,probe.log,smoke.log,compile.log}).","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:54:04Z"},{"id":2350,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","author":"RedLotus","text":"RedLotus revalidation pass 2026-03-14: confirmed code path in crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs is present as described by WhiteFox, including command_uses_rch / requires_rch_offload helpers and policy.rch.heavy_compute require-approval for non-human SendText heavy cargo without an rch wrapper. Fresh live infra evidence is contradictory but improved versus the original blocker: 'rch check' and 'rch status --jobs' report healthy workers, 'rch workers probe --all' fails, and 'TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help' still completes remotely. A targeted remote test run for command_uses_rch_detects_common_prefix_shapes is currently active on worker vmi1153651 (RCH queue build #114) using TMPDIR=/tmp and CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/redlotus-ft3681t732; final pass/fail result still pending at comment time.","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:07:34Z"},{"id":2351,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","author":"RedLotus","text":"RedLotus follow-up 2026-03-14: the original topology-drift blocker is no longer the full story. I ran two additional rch-only targeted remote test attempts against command_uses_rch_detects_common_prefix_shapes on worker vmi1153651 with TMPDIR=/tmp. Attempt A: default features, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/redlotus-ft3681t732 (RCH build #114). Attempt B: --no-default-features, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/redlotus-ft3681t732-nodefault (RCH build #128). Both synced and executed remotely on vmi1153651; both advanced through long compiles (A reached frankenterm-core with warning output from web.rs, B reached frankenterm-core plus criterion/zstd) and then remained attached to a sleeping ssh wrapper for many minutes without ever emitting a test result. rch queue continued to show the builds active until I canceled them. Separate control command  still succeeds remotely. Net: targeted remote cargo test execution is still infra-degraded/hanging even on a worker that can execute remote cargo commands, so this bead remains validation-blocked by rch behavior rather than code findings.","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:18:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.4","title":"[ops] Chaos + scale validation harness (1k-pane/connector stress)","description":"Build a rigorous resilience harness covering large pane counts, bursty connector traffic, policy churn, and partial outages to validate SLOs before migration cutover.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Scale tests model realistic swarm + connector load. 2. Chaos scenarios cover key failure classes and recovery. 3. Pass/fail gates are explicit for release decisions.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","notes":"Implemented chaos_scale_harness.rs (~943 lines). 26 property tests + 2 unit tests in proptest_chaos_scale_harness.rs covering serde roundtrips, scale profile invariants, harness run invariants, failure injection effects, SLO evaluation logic, report serialization.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkForge","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:36.654720Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T04:45:32.601329Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T04:45:32.601329Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","ops","performance","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:28.470529Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:36.654720Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:27.578519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:28.013063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-ml1b","title":"Agent skills/docs: how to use wa via robot+MCP (swarm playbooks)","description":"# Task: Agent skills/docs (swarm playbooks)

## Goal
Write self-contained guidance for agent swarms to use wa effectively.

## Topics
- When to use `wa robot` vs MCP.
- Canonical flows:
  - check state
  - read text tail
  - search history
  - handle unhandled events
  - run workflows
- Safety expectations:
  - policy gates
  - avoid alt-screen
  - verify after sending

## Deliverables
- `skills/` docs (or equivalent) that can be copied into agent system prompts.

## Validation / tests
Agent playbooks must not drift.

- Prefer to keep examples aligned with:
  - robot JSON schemas (field names)
  - E2E scripts (canonical flows)

- If feasible, add a small CI check that:
  - runs a couple “playbook” robot commands against fixtures
  - asserts outputs are schema-parseable

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new agent can operate wa without human coaching.


## Testing
- Drift prevention:
  - Add a CI check (or unit test) that validates embedded robot JSON snippets against schemas.
  - Ensure docs examples are generated/updated from fixtures where possible to avoid bit-rot.
","notes":"Completed swarm playbooks docs + drift guard. Added docs/swarm-playbook.md (robot vs MCP guidance, canonical control loop, event triage mutation flow, workflow/send verification, safety rules, prompt snippet). Linked from README.md MCP section. Added docs smoke test smoke_robot_playbook_commands_emit_json_envelopes in crates/wa/tests/docs_smoke.rs to validate core playbook robot commands emit parseable JSON envelopes with boolean ok field. Validation performed: cargo fmt -p wa --check (pass); runtime smoke using existing target/debug/wa with isolated WA_WORKSPACE + WA_WEZTERM_CLI=/nonexistent/wezterm confirmed parseable JSON envelopes for robot state/events/workflow list. Workspace currently has unrelated wa-core compile blockers (E0603 in crates/wa-core/src/undo.rs, E0609 in crates/wa-core/src/storage.rs) preventing full cargo test/check/clippy in this mixed-agent branch.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:42:01.859702463Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.217664Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:25.000942122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-mw44","title":"Add alt-screen detection via escape sequence parsing in pattern engine","description":"## Overview

Implement alt-screen (alternate screen buffer) detection by parsing terminal escape sequences in the captured output stream, eliminating the need for Lua's \`pane:is_alt_screen_active()\`.

## Background

Terminal applications (vim, less, htop, etc.) switch to the alternate screen buffer using standardized escape sequences:

\`\`\`
Enter alt-screen: ESC [ ? 1049 h   (smcup - start mode cup)
Leave alt-screen: ESC [ ? 1049 l   (rmcup - reset mode cup)
\`\`\`

These sequences are emitted by the application and captured in wa's output stream. By detecting them, we know the alt-screen state without asking WezTerm via Lua.

## Technical Details

### Escape Sequence Variants
Some terminals/apps use alternative sequences:
\`\`\`
Primary (most common):
  ESC [ ? 1049 h    — enter
  ESC [ ? 1049 l    — leave

Alternative (xterm compatibility):
  ESC [ ? 47 h      — enter (older)
  ESC [ ? 47 l      — leave (older)

Combined with cursor save/restore:
  ESC [ ? 1049 h ESC [ ? 1 h   — enter + application cursor keys
\`\`\`

### Implementation Location

Add to **crates/wa-core/src/patterns.rs** or create new **crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs**:

\`\`\`rust
/// Tracks alt-screen state by parsing escape sequences
pub struct ScreenStateTracker {
    /// Current alt-screen state per pane
    alt_screen_active: HashMap<u64, bool>,
}

impl ScreenStateTracker {
    /// Process captured output, update alt-screen state
    pub fn process_output(&mut self, pane_id: u64, output: &[u8]) {
        // Scan for ESC [ ? 1049 h/l sequences
        // Update self.alt_screen_active[pane_id]
    }
    
    /// Query current alt-screen state
    pub fn is_alt_screen(&self, pane_id: u64) -> bool {
        self.alt_screen_active.get(&pane_id).copied().unwrap_or(false)
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Points

1. **Ingest loop** (ingest.rs): After capturing pane output, pass to ScreenStateTracker
2. **Pattern detection** (patterns.rs): Can use alt-screen state in rule matching
3. **IPC server** (ipc.rs): StatusUpdate can report alt-screen from tracker

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ScreenStateTracker struct implemented
- [ ] Detects ESC[?1049h as alt-screen enter
- [ ] Detects ESC[?1049l as alt-screen leave
- [ ] Handles sequences split across capture boundaries
- [ ] Unit tests with various escape sequence formats
- [ ] Integration with ingest loop
- [ ] Benchmark showing minimal overhead

## Test Cases

1. **Basic enter/leave**: Single ESC[?1049h followed by ESC[?1049l
2. **Partial sequence**: Sequence split across two output chunks
3. **Nested/repeated**: Multiple enters without leave (state stays active)
4. **Mixed content**: Normal text interspersed with sequences
5. **Alternative sequences**: Test ESC[?47h/l variants
6. **No sequences**: Normal output doesn't change state

## Performance Considerations

- Use memchr or Aho-Corasick for efficient ESC scanning
- Avoid regex for this (too slow for every output chunk)
- Track state change timestamps for debugging

## Files to Modify

- crates/wa-core/src/lib.rs — add mod screen_state
- NEW: crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs — ScreenStateTracker
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs — integrate tracker
- crates/wa-core/src/ipc.rs — StatusUpdate uses tracker

## References

- XTerm control sequences: https://invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html
- ECMA-48 (ANSI escape codes)
